// Project 04 of 07

Database Design & SQL Coursework

MAX-506 Database Design and SQL · Illinois Institute of Technology · Fall 2025

Group Project 13-Entity Schema 3NF Normalized SQL Queries
Designed and implemented a fully normalized relational database for the Richard Ivey School of Business doctoral program — replacing a manual, error-prone tracking system with a structured schema capable of generating real-time Student Progress and Enrollment reports.

// 01 · Problem Context

What Linda Had — and What the Database Needed to Solve

The Situation
The doctoral program at Ivey relied on manual paper files and word-processor reports. Dual grade sources caused reconciliation errors, incomplete requirements had no systematic tracking, and there was no mechanism to flag 7-year deadline breaches — all while enrollment kept growing.
The Objective
Design a relational database to replace the manual process — modeling students, faculty, area groups, courses, degree requirements, enrollment statuses, and dissertation milestones — all within a normalized schema supporting two live reports: Student Progress and Enrollment.

// 02 · Design Lifecycle

4-Stage Design Journey

01
Conceptual
Entity identification, ER diagram, M:N bridge tables, relationship cardinalities
02
Logical
Normalization to 3NF — eliminating partial and transitive dependencies across all 13 tables
03
Physical
DDL in dependency order, domains, assertions, views, and DCL access control
04
Validation
Dual grades, dual fulfillment, 3-term incomplete rule, non-course requirements

// 03 · Schema Overview

13-Entity Relational Schema

Three many-to-many bridge tables, two self-referencing foreign keys on Course (prerequisite and supersession), and one history table extracted from Student to satisfy 1NF.

StudentCentral entity — identity, enrollment, advisor FKs, 7-year deadline
FacultyAdvisor, thesis advisor, and committee member — one source record
Area_GroupSeven doctoral divisions referenced by both students and requirements
RequirementProgram obligations with boolean flags: IsGraded, IsWeighted, CanBeWaived
CourseSelf-referencing FKs for prerequisite and supersession history
TermAnchors scheduling and incomplete-grade tracking across tables
Course_OfferingOne scheduled section = Course + Term + Instructor
EnrollmentLinks Student to Course_Offering; tied back to Student_Requirement
Student_RequirementCore tracking entity — grade, status, waiver, and incomplete term FKs
Area_Group_RequirementBridge: M:N between area groups and requirements
Student_Status_HistoryFull timeline of enrollment status changes — 1NF compliant
ThesisSeparate lifecycle — proposal, defence dates, status, international flag
Thesis_CommitteeBridge: Faculty × Thesis with role, date appointed, and date removed

// 04 · Project Presentation

Group 3 — Full Slide Deck

Presented to the class covering the full 4-stage design journey: problem context, entity identification, ER diagram, normalization to 3NF, DDL implementation, and validation edge cases.
